title: Ford and Lincoln Recall 741,195 Trucks and SUVs That May Roll Away
summary: The recall affects the Ford Expedition, Ford Explorer, Ford F-150, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Navigator.
whatHappening: Ford and Lincoln are recalling over 741,000 trucks and SUVs due to a defect that could cause vehicles to roll away unexpectedly. The recall affects five popular models: Ford Expedition, Explorer, F-150, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Navigator.
whoInvolved: Ford Motor Company and its luxury division Lincoln are issuing the recall.
whyMatters: The rollaway risk poses a serious safety hazard to owners and bystanders, potentially causing accidents, injuries, or property damage. Owners of affected vehicles should have the issue addressed immediately through authorized dealers.

title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 BMW X5 Hybrid
summary: See the interior of the 2027 BMW X5 Hybrid, now in its fifth generation.
whatHappening: BMW is unveiling interior photos of its redesigned 2027 X5 Hybrid, marking the luxury SUV's entry into its fifth generation. The photos showcase the updated cabin design and features for the new model year.
whoInvolved: BMW is the automaker behind the 2027 X5 Hybrid, with coverage from Car and Driver.
whyMatters: The fifth-generation X5 Hybrid represents BMW's latest technology and design language in the competitive luxury SUV segment. New interior photos give potential buyers and enthusiasts an early look at what to expect from the refreshed model.

title: Land Rover Recalls 250,857 SUVs over Potential Airbag Issue
summary: The Land Rover Defender, Discovery, and Range Rover are all being recalled because the driver's airbag may not deploy as intended.
whatHappening: Land Rover is recalling 250,857 SUVs due to a potential airbag deployment failure. The defect affects driver-side airbags in the Defender, Discovery, and Range Rover models.
whoInvolved: Land Rover is conducting the recall across multiple vehicle lines.
whyMatters: A non-deploying airbag significantly increases injury risk in frontal collisions. Affected owners should contact Land Rover immediately to have their vehicles inspected and repaired.

title: Mitsubishi Teases the Return of an Iconic Feature on the New Montero
summary: The Montero—or Pajero, depending on where you live—will debut in Asia later this year and launch in North America closer to 2030.
whatHappening: Mitsubishi is bringing back an iconic feature on the redesigned Montero SUV. The new model will debut in Asia later this year with a North American launch expected around 2030.
whoInvolved: Mitsubishi Motors is leading the Montero's revival.
whyMatters: The Montero's return signals Mitsubishi's commitment to reviving a legendary nameplate that has been absent from markets. This move could help the automaker compete in the competitive SUV segment and reconnect with nostalgic consumers who remember the original model's off-road capabilities.

title: Jaguar XJ220 and XJ220-C Reimagined as Modern Masterpieces
summary: Legendary designer Ian Callum, the brain behind nearly 20 years of Jaguar design, revealed his reinterpretation of the iconic XJ220.
whatHappening: Legendary designer Ian Callum has reimagined the iconic Jaguar XJ220 as a modern masterpiece. His reinterpretation includes both the XJ220 and XJ220-C variants.
whoInvolved: Ian Callum, the designer behind nearly 20 years of Jaguar design, led this project.
whyMatters: This reinterpretation bridges classic automotive heritage with contemporary design sensibilities, potentially influencing how iconic vehicles are reimagined for modern audiences. Callum's vision demonstrates how legendary designs can remain relevant through thoughtful modernization.

title: 2026 Nissan Frontier Celebrates America with Free Patriotic Badge
summary: Not only is Nissan honoring our country's 250th birthday, but it has also now built 1 million Frontiers at its factory in Canton, Mississippi.
whatHappening: Nissan is giving the 2026 Frontier a free patriotic badge to celebrate America's 250th birthday. The automaker has simultaneously hit a production milestone of 1 million Frontiers at its Canton, Mississippi factory.
whoInvolved: Nissan and its Canton, Mississippi manufacturing facility.
whyMatters: The milestone demonstrates Nissan's long-term commitment to domestic production and the strength of the Frontier nameplate in the American truck market. The patriotic branding ties the achievement to national pride during a significant anniversary year.

title: Two U.K. Companies Used Their Imagination, and Lotus Inspiration, for This Concept Collab
summary: Watt Electric Vehicles brings functional engineering to the table, while Avant Design leverages the use of VR in styling.
whatHappening: Two U.K. companies are collaborating on a Lotus-inspired concept vehicle that blends engineering innovation with cutting-edge design technology. Watt Electric Vehicles handles the functional engineering while Avant Design uses VR to guide the styling process.
whoInvolved: Watt Electric Vehicles, Avant Design, and Lotus are the primary collaborators on this concept project.
whyMatters: This partnership demonstrates how combining traditional automotive engineering with virtual reality design tools can accelerate concept development and create more imaginative vehicle designs. The collaboration signals how British companies are leveraging advanced technology to compete in the evolving EV market.

title: Shane van Gisbergen Wins NASCAR's Last Road Course Race of 2026 at Sonoma
summary: The road course ace now sits at a 50% win rate on road courses and is just one win short of Jeff Gordon's NASCAR Cup road course record.
whatHappening: Shane van Gisbergen won NASCAR's final road course race of 2026 at Sonoma. The victory brings his road course win rate to 50% and puts him one win away from Jeff Gordon's Cup Series road course record.
whoInvolved: Shane van Gisbergen (driver) and Jeff Gordon (record holder).
whyMatters: Van Gisbergen is establishing himself as one of NASCAR's elite road course specialists. He's on the verge of claiming one of the sport's most prestigious individual records.

title: This 1985 Ferrari 288 GTO Is Bring a Trailer's 250,000th Auction
summary: Bidding for the Ferrari supercar is already into the millions.
whatHappening: A 1985 Ferrari 288 GTO is up for auction on Bring a Trailer, marking the platform's 250,000th auction. Bidding has already climbed into the millions.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer, the online auction platform, and the owner of this rare Ferrari supercar.
whyMatters: This milestone represents Bring a Trailer's scale as a major player in the collector car market. The 288 GTO's high bidding underscores continued strong demand for rare, vintage Ferraris.

title: How George Russell Beat a Yellow Flag to Take Pole in Austria – While Kimi Antonelli Got It Wrong
summary: George Russell passed Max Verstappen's crash scene and yet still earned pole position.
whatHappening: George Russell secured pole position at the Austrian Grand Prix despite passing through a yellow flag zone created by Max Verstappen's crash. Kimi Antonelli failed to capitalize on the same opportunity, losing out on a better qualifying position.
whoInvolved: Mercedes driver George Russell, Red Bull driver Max Verstappen, and Williams driver Kimi Antonelli are the central figures in this qualifying battle.
whyMatters: Russell's pole-position lap demonstrates precise execution under pressure and potentially gives Mercedes a crucial advantage heading into the race. The contrast with Antonelli's approach highlights the fine margins that separate success from failure in elite motorsport qualifying.

title: Mazda Was Forced to Change the Miata Because It Was ... Too Noisy?
summary: You wouldn’t expect the tiny roadster to break noise requirements, and yet, the issue apparently arose.
whatHappening: Mazda had to redesign the Miata roadster because it was exceeding noise regulations. The tiny sports car's engine was apparently too loud to meet legal requirements.
whoInvolved: Mazda is addressing regulatory compliance issues with its iconic Miata model.
whyMatters: This reveals how even small, lightweight vehicles must comply with increasingly strict environmental and noise standards. It highlights the engineering challenges automakers face in meeting global regulations without compromising performance character.

title: Ian Callum Has a Vision of a Reimagined Jaguar XJ220
summary: The legendary designer behind modern Jaguars has unveiled a striking new take on the iconic supercar.
whatHappening: Ian Callum, the legendary Jaguar designer, has revealed a reimagined vision of the iconic XJ220 supercar. His modern take presents a striking design update to the classic model.
whoInvolved: Ian Callum, the designer responsible for modern Jaguar vehicles, is the central figure in this design unveiling.
whyMatters: This reimagining signals continued interest in reviving Jaguar's most celebrated performance vehicles and shows how legendary designers are reinterpreting automotive icons for contemporary audiences. It reflects the ongoing conversation about blending classic design heritage with modern automotive aesthetics.

title: How to Buy or Lease a New Car
summary: We walk through the steps a smart car buyer should take to remove intimidation from the process and end up with the best deal on the right new car.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is breaking down the intimidating car buying and leasing process into manageable steps. The guide aims to help consumers navigate dealership negotiations and secure the best possible deal.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver provides the guidance; the audience is prospective car buyers and lessees.
whyMatters: Most people find car buying stressful and complex, often resulting in overpaying or making poor decisions. A clear roadmap empowers consumers to approach dealerships with confidence and save money.

title: Porsche Leads JD Power Study of Initial Quality, Ford Tops Mainstream Brands
summary: The 911 doesn't just deliver thrills behind the wheel; it also leads the industry in fewest problems during the first part of ownership.
whatHappening: Porsche's 911 has topped JD Power's Initial Quality Study, showing the fewest problems in early ownership. Ford is leading among mainstream vehicle brands in the same ranking.
whoInvolved: Porsche, Ford, and JD Power (the research firm conducting the quality study).
whyMatters: Initial quality rankings influence buyer confidence and can impact brand reputation and sales. High marks in early ownership suggest better reliability and owner satisfaction, giving Porsche and Ford competitive advantages in their respective market segments.

title: How to Get the Best Price for Trading In Your Car
summary: Learning about the trade-in process can help you find out how to price your trade-in and determine whether this strategy is best for you.
whatHappening: Car owners are learning how to navigate the trade-in process to secure better deals on their vehicles. Understanding pricing strategies and evaluation methods is becoming essential for anyone selling a car to a dealership.
whoInvolved: Car buyers and dealerships are the primary parties in trade-in transactions, with guidance from automotive experts at publications like Car and Driver.
whyMatters: Getting the best trade-in price can significantly impact the total cost of purchasing a new vehicle. Knowing how to properly evaluate and price a trade-in helps consumers make informed financial decisions and avoid leaving money on the table.

title: Parade of Absurdity: The Fantastical Concept Car Caravan of Luigi Colani
summary: Automorrow ’89 was a remarkable combination of substance and showmanship.
whatHappening: Luigi Colani is showcasing a collection of fantastical concept cars in a parade that blends artistic vision with automotive engineering. Automorrow '89 demonstrates how far designers will push the boundaries of vehicle aesthetics and functionality.
whoInvolved: Luigi Colani, a renowned designer, is leading this concept car exhibition covered by Road & Track.
whyMatters: The event represents a rare intersection of substance and showmanship in automotive design, influencing how manufacturers think about future vehicle concepts. Colani's work pushes creative boundaries and challenges conventional thinking about car design.

title: A Resurgent Lewis Hamilton Isn’t Thinking About the F1 World Championship Just Yet
summary: The Ferrari ace isn’t getting carried away, even after his win in Spain.
whatHappening: Lewis Hamilton has won in Spain, showing a resurgence in form with Ferrari. Despite the strong performance, he's maintaining a measured mindset about championship prospects.
whoInvolved: Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari are at the center of this story.
whyMatters: Hamilton's renewed competitiveness signals Ferrari's growing strength in the F1 season, while his cautious approach suggests the championship battle remains wide open with significant racing ahead.

title: Volkswagen CEO Is Looking to Lay Off 100,000 Employees: Report
summary: The plan supposedly includes the shuttering of four factories, too.
whatHappening: Volkswagen's CEO is planning to lay off approximately 100,000 employees and shut down four factories. The restructuring represents a major shift for the German automaker.
whoInvolved: Volkswagen and its CEO are leading this restructuring effort.
whyMatters: The layoffs would significantly reshape Volkswagen's workforce and production capacity, affecting thousands of employees and potentially impacting the company's manufacturing footprint. This signals major challenges in the automotive industry, likely tied to the shift toward electric vehicles and changing market demands.

title: Florida Law Puts End to NASCAR Founder's Great-Grandson’s Controversial Motorcycle YouTube Channel
summary: The channel featured video of motorcycles engaging in stunt riding and traveling at indicated speeds of 150-plus miles an hour.
whatHappening: A Florida law has shut down a controversial motorcycle YouTube channel run by a NASCAR founder's great-grandson. The channel featured extreme stunt riding and footage of bikes traveling over 150 mph.
whoInvolved: A descendant of NASCAR's founding family and Florida state lawmakers.
whyMatters: The action highlights growing legal pushback against dangerous content on social media platforms. It raises questions about platform accountability and whether family legacy provides immunity from traffic and safety laws.

title: 2030's New RWD-Only Rules for Le Mans Hypercars Are a Must for the Continued Health of the Series
summary: Top-tier sports car racing is about to be more accessible than ever, something worth the cost of the more innovative all-wheel drive hybrids racing today.
whatHappening: Le Mans Hypercars are switching to rear-wheel-drive-only regulations starting in 2030. This significant rule change will eliminate the complex all-wheel drive hybrid systems currently dominating top-tier sports car racing.
whoInvolved: Le Mans organizers and FIA (Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile), along with major hypercar manufacturers competing in the series.
whyMatters: The RWD-only mandate will make hypercar racing more accessible to manufacturers by reducing technological complexity and costs, while potentially sacrificing some performance innovation. This democratization could attract new competitors and strengthen the long-term health of the series.

title: When Your Car Lease Is Over, What Should You Do Next?
summary: Should you buy your car at lease end, or start a new relationship with a new vehicle? In this time of higher prices and scarcity, it's important to think it through.
whatHappening: Drivers are facing a critical decision as their car leases end during a period of elevated vehicle prices and limited inventory. They must choose between purchasing their leased vehicle or starting fresh with a new one.
whoInvolved: Car lessees and the automotive industry, including dealers and manufacturers managing end-of-lease vehicles.
whyMatters: In today's expensive and competitive auto market, making the wrong choice at lease end could cost thousands of dollars or leave drivers stuck with unfavorable options. The decision requires careful financial and practical consideration given current market conditions.

title: Pearley: A Lot to Love About NASCAR's Weekend at Naval Base Coronado. But It Could Be Better.
summary: NASCAR in Coronado reminds me of Formula 5000 in Long Beach 50 years ago.
whatHappening: NASCAR is racing at Naval Base Coronado for a weekend event. The unique venue offers nostalgic appeal but has room for improvement.
whoInvolved: NASCAR and the venue at Naval Base Coronado.
whyMatters: The event harks back to historic racing moments like Formula 5000 in Long Beach decades ago, signaling NASCAR's willingness to experiment with unconventional venues. However, execution gaps suggest the series needs to refine how it stages such events to maximize their potential.

title: 2026 Tesla Model Y vs. 2026 Toyota bZ Comparison Test: Two Bestsellers, One Winner
summary: Can Toyota's refreshed and renamed EV SUV topple Tesla's similarly improved global superstar?
whatHappening: Car and Driver is pitting the redesigned 2026 Tesla Model Y against Toyota's refreshed and rebranded electric SUV in a head-to-head comparison. The test determines which bestselling EV SUV comes out on top.
whoInvolved: Tesla and Toyota, two automotive giants with dominant EV market positions.
whyMatters: This matchup matters because both vehicles represent the mainstream EV market's biggest players, and the winner signals which automaker better delivers on performance, features, and value for mass-market electric SUV buyers.

title: George Russell Seizes Controversial F1 Austrian GP Pole Following Max Verstappen's Crash
summary: George Russell backed off in the final sector after saying he saw a single yellow flag following Max Verstappen's crash in turn 9 and still completed the best lap of the day in qualifying for the Austrian Grand Prix. The Stewards say no further investigation is needed.
whatHappening: George Russell has secured pole position for the Austrian Grand Prix after backing off in the final sector following Max Verstappen's crash at turn 9. Russell still managed to post the fastest lap time despite the yellow flag warning.
whoInvolved: George Russell, Max Verstappen, and the F1 Stewards are the key parties in this qualifying session.
whyMatters: The pole position is now controversial because Russell benefited from Verstappen's crash while maintaining track position, raising questions about fair competition. The Stewards' decision not to investigate further means Russell's advantage will stand despite the circumstances surrounding the session.

title: Morgan is Offering Its Six-Cylinder Midsummer as a Limited-Run Coupe
summary: Only nine of this jaw-dropping car will be sold.
whatHappening: Morgan is launching the Midsummer, a six-cylinder limited-run coupe with only nine units available for sale. This marks an exclusive new offering from the British automaker.
whoInvolved: Morgan Motor Company is behind this vehicle release.
whyMatters: The extreme scarcity—just nine cars—makes this a highly coveted collector's piece for enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices. It showcases Morgan's commitment to hand-crafted, limited-production vehicles that command attention in the luxury automotive market.

title: Frrom the Archive: 2001 Toyota Camry Solara TRD Tested
summary: Supercharged and lowered, it's a much quicker ride to the golf course.
whatHappening: Car and Driver revisits a modified 2001 Toyota Camry Solara TRD that's been supercharged and lowered for enhanced performance. The upgrades transform the vehicle into a significantly quicker ride.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver tested the modified Toyota Camry Solara TRD.
whyMatters: The archive piece showcases how aftermarket modifications can dramatically improve a sedan's performance capabilities. It highlights the tuning potential of early 2000s Toyota models for enthusiasts seeking to enhance their vehicles.

title: Redemption at Last? 2001 Pontiac Aztek on Bring a Trailer
summary: Think of it this way: You'd surely have the only one at Cars and Coffee.
whatHappening: A 2001 Pontiac Aztek is being listed for sale on Bring a Trailer, the online auction platform. The notorious vehicle is being positioned as a unique collectible rather than a punchline.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver is reporting on the listing; Bring a Trailer is facilitating the sale of the Pontiac Aztek.
whyMatters: The Aztek has long been mocked as one of the worst-designed cars ever made, so its appearance on a premium collector car platform signals a potential cultural shift toward embracing automotive oddities. It highlights how time and nostalgia can transform a failed product into a sought-after curiosity.

title: View Photos of the 2001 Four Seat Convertible Comparison Test
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 2001 Toyota Camry Solara SLE, Ford Mustang GT, Chrysler Sebring Limited, Mitsubishi Eclipse Spyder GT and Chevrolet Camaro Z28 from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is showcasing a four-seat convertible comparison test from 2001, featuring detailed photos of five popular models from every angle. The visual gallery captures the interior and exterior design of each vehicle.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver conducted the test featuring Toyota, Ford, Chrysler, Mitsubishi, and Chevrolet convertible models.
whyMatters: This comparison provides potential buyers with a comprehensive visual reference to evaluate the design, styling, and features of top convertible options in the early 2000s market. The detailed photography helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions among competing models.

title: From the Archive: 2001 Four-Seat Convertible Comparison Test
summary: Chevy Camaro vs. Chrysler Sebring vs. Ford Mustang vs. Mitsubishi Eclipse vs. Toyota Camry Solara.
whatHappening: Car and Driver revisits a classic 2001 comparison test of four-seat convertibles, pitting five popular models against each other to determine which deserves the top spot.
whoInvolved: Chevrolet, Chrysler, Ford, Mitsubishi, and Toyota competed with their Camaro, Sebring, Mustang, Eclipse, and Camry Solara models respectively.
whyMatters: This archival test captures a pivotal moment in the convertible market when American muscle cars competed directly with Japanese imports. It provides historical context for how these iconic nameplates have evolved over two decades.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2026 Tesla Model Y
summary: See the exterior of the 2026 Tesla Model Y from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is showcasing exclusive exterior photos of Tesla's upcoming 2026 Model Y. The images provide a comprehensive 360-degree view of the vehicle's redesigned styling.
whoInvolved: Tesla and Car and Driver are the key players in this automotive reveal.
whyMatters: The 2026 Model Y refresh signals Tesla's continued evolution of its best-selling vehicle and gives potential buyers their first look at the next generation's design direction. This preview builds anticipation ahead of the official launch.
